description: Collected published estimates of the hidden / external (mainly environmental, plus health and subsidy) costs of beef and a beef burger that are not reflected in the retail price. Each study's headline figure is a value, qualified by study, entity, period and cost scope. Figures come from peer-reviewed life-cycle / true-cost-accounting studies and from two widely-cited retail demonstrations (Penny supermarket 2020, US meat-subsidy analysis 2015). Currency and per-unit basis differ between studies and are kept as separate qualified values rather than merged - see each value's qualifier set and source.

words.name: burger
words.description: ground-beef patty in a bun; the prototypical fast-food beef product

words.name: beef
words.description: meat of cattle; the food product with the highest external cost per kg in true-cost studies

words.name: meat
words.description: edible animal flesh; here mostly the conventional-production aggregate

words.name: patty
words.description: a single formed portion of ground meat

words.name: cost
words.description: monetary amount; qualified below as retail, external or true

words.name: price
words.description: amount actually paid by the consumer at retail

words.name: retail
words.description: the price tag a consumer pays, excluding externalities

words.name: external
words.description: cost borne by third parties / society, not by the buyer or seller (an externality)

words.name: true
words.description: retail price plus internalised external cost; the full social cost

words.name: hidden
words.description: synonym used in popular reporting for the external, non-priced cost

words.name: environmental
words.description: external cost scope: greenhouse gases, fertiliser / nutrient pollution, energy, land, water

words.name: health
words.description: external cost scope: diet-related disease attributed to the product

words.name: subsidy
words.description: government transfer that lowers the price paid below the cost of production

words.name: increase
words.description: the amount or share by which a price would rise

words.name: share
words.description: a fraction, here usually expressed as a percentage

words.name: EUR
words.description: euro

words.name: USD
words.description: US dollar

words.name: CHF
words.description: Swiss franc

words.name: percent
words.description: share expressed per hundred

words.name: kg
words.description: kilogram

words.name: g
words.description: gram

words.name: minced
words.description: cut up or ground into very small pieces, typically in a machine

words.name: lower
words.description: the bottom digit or minimum value in a range estimate

words.name: upper
words.description: the top digit or maximum value in a range estimate

words.name: Big Mac
words.description: McDonald's beef burger used as the unit in the US true-cost / subsidy commentary

words.name: Beyond Burger
words.description: plant-based patty used as the low-externality comparison in Reisinger et al. 2025

sources.name: Springmann meat tax 2022
sources.description: Funke F, Mattauch L, ... Edenhofer O (2022). Toward Optimal Meat Pricing: Is It Time to Tax Meat Consumption? Review of Environmental Economics and Policy. External cost of beef from climate + nutrient pollution US$5.75-9.17/kg.

sources.name: Michalke TCA 2023
sources.description: Michalke A, Stein L, Fichtner R, Gaugler T, Stoll-Kleemann S (2023). True cost accounting of organic and conventional food production. J. Cleaner Production 408:137134. Conventional meat externality EUR 4.42/kg; beef highest of all products.

sources.name: Penny true cost 2020
sources.description: University of Augsburg (Gaugler T et al.) for Penny / Rewe Group, 2020 retail demonstration. Conventional minced meat true price EUR 7.62 vs retail EUR 2.79 (+173%); environmental scope (GHG, over-fertilisation, energy).

sources.name: Arrigoni Italy LCA 2023
sources.description: Arrigoni A, Marveggio D, ... (2023). Environmental and health-related external costs of meat consumption in Italy. Sci. Total Environ. External cost of beef EUR 1.3 per 100 g pre-ingestion.

sources.name: Perotti Switzerland 2019
sources.description: Perotti (2019), 2018 analysis of ~100 externality types for Switzerland. Beef external costs amount to 125% of the price paid by the consumer.

sources.name: Reisinger PBMA 2025
sources.description: Reisinger A et al. (2025). Quantifying ... Plant-Based Meat Alternatives ... vs Beef. Foods 15(11):1949. Monetised total dietary risk: beef patty USD 1.63 vs Beyond Burger USD 0.16; Beyond Burger ~9% of beef patty external cost.

sources.name: US meat subsidy commentary 2015
sources.description: Gillette D, citing 2015 analysis: US federal meat & dairy subsidies (~US$38bn/yr) hide cost; un-subsidised Big Mac ~US$13 vs ~US$5 retail; ground beef ~US$30 vs ~US$5 per pound. Subsidy-only scope, not full externality.

values.description: lower bound of average external cost of beef from climate change and nutrient pollution; biodiversity and diet-health would add substantially
upper bound of the same range
average external cost of conventional meat across the studied German products; beef generates the highest of all 22 products
retail price of 500 g conventional mixed minced meat (the 500 g basis is the qualifier g, value is for the 500 g pack)
environmental true price of the same 500 g pack
implied environmental mark-up on conventional minced meat
external (environmental + health) cost of 100 g of beef pre-ingestion; poultry/pork 0.5 EUR per 100 g for contrast
beef external costs as a share of the price the consumer pays (the highest of the animal products studied)
monetised total dietary risk (health externality) of one 3-oz cooked beef patty
monetised total dietary risk of one Beyond Burger patty; ~9% of the beef patty's combined external cost
approximate un-subsidised price (subsidy scope only, before wider environmental/health externalities)

formulas.name: external cost (formula)
formulas.description: the external (non-priced) cost is the full social true cost minus the retail price the consumer actually pays

calc-validation.note: 13 un-subsidised - 5 retail (US meat subsidy commentary 2015, subsidy scope only)
